design_philosophy.core_loop: Vertically scroll through military-themed stages while managing limited bomb weapons and firepower pickups; destroy enemy waves and reach runway checkpoints to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward between conserving bombs for dense enemy formations vs. using them to clear screen bullets; firepower loss penalty on hit creates recovery gameplay
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot a military biplane through war-torn landscapes, commanding devastating bomb strikes to clear overwhelming enemy fire
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Stress-relief bomb mechanic prioritized over constant shooting; checkpoint system punishes death with firepower reset rather than stage restart; game designed for 'tricks and knowledge accumulated through gameplay' rather than pure execution

difficulty.lives_system: Three starting lives; bonus lives awarded at 50000 points and thereafter
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade credit insertion system; checkpoint restarts at reached runway positions
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint system at each runway; death resets firepower to initial state but allows restart at reached checkpoint rather than stage beginning
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Second loop increases difficulty significantly; bullet speed increases in higher loops but normalizes after overflow; firepower penalty on hit creates dynamic difficulty scaling based on player survival

progression.upgrade_system: Firepower increased by destroying colored enemy plane waves (S icons); bombs accumulated by destroying ground units (B icons); bomb count multiplies for 3000 points upon each runway landing
progression.unlock_mechanism: Five stages unlock sequentially; completing final stage triggers second loop with increased enemy difficulty and bullet speeds
replayability_hooks: Second loop with higher difficulty for continued play
Score chasing with runway bonuses and point multipliers

world_building_techniques: Military-themed enemy roster (tanks, battleships, aircraft, artillery)
Progression through war-torn landscapes with scrolling backgrounds
Aesthetic inspired by Apocalypse Now (1979) war film for emotional immersion
Thailand-inspired scenery in backgrounds to convey geographical context

faction_indicators: Player vs. military forces (tanks, battleships, enemy planes, artillery)
danger_signals: Enemy bullet density increases with loop progression
Firepower penalty on player hit creates vulnerability spirals
Checkpoint loss means restart from runway position, not stage start
environmental_storytelling: Scrolling landscapes shift from land to sea to air combat zones
Enemy unit variety signals escalating military opposition
Runway landings serve as military base/safe zone indicators

sources_used: Wikipedia - Flying Shark (1987 arcade original; gameplay, development philosophy, checkpoint system, bomb mechanics, difficulty scaling)
The Cutting Room Floor - Sky Shark (NES) (developer credits, release date verification)
